title: Evangelion Artbook Comparisons
wikitext:
[]: Evangelion Artbook Comparisons             Quote Post by alexprevity » Sat Dec 01, 2012 6:59 pm Can anybody tell me what the difference is between these artbooks? -Der Mond -Die Sterne -Chronicle: Illustrations -Newtype 100% Collection Christmas is right around the corner and it'd be nice to have some Evangelion art to flip through. As I've noticed, some are more expensive/harder to come across than others. Could I get some insight on the difference in content between these books? Thanks!

[]: Re: Evangelion Artbook Comparisons             Quote Post by Grand Duke of Yashima » Sat Dec 01, 2012 10:48 pm alexprevity wrote: Can anybody tell me what the difference is between these artbooks? -Der Mond -Die Sterne -Chronicle: Illustrations -Newtype 100% Collection

[]: Re: Evangelion Artbook Comparisons             Quote Post by cyharding » Sun Dec 02, 2012 12:03 am Grand Duke of Yashima wrote: I think that you can get all of the others in English.

[]:             Quote Post by alexprevity » Sun Dec 02, 2012 12:38 am Thanks for the tip! The Newtype Collections are pretty easy to find (used, at least). I'll look into Chronicle as well.

[]:             Quote Post by Blue Monday » Sun Dec 02, 2012 12:53 am All the aforementioned publications are fantastic, and you should get them if you can. I don't have the Newtype Collection though - Will have to get my hands on it eventually, I guess.

[]: Re: Evangelion Artbook Comparisons             Quote Post by Hyper Shinchan » Sun Dec 02, 2012 1:12 am cyharding wrote: I'm sure there are other languages printed, but not in English.

[]:             Quote Post by Grand Duke of Yashima » Sun Dec 02, 2012 1:17 am Cyharding you are correct and it's my mistake. Die Sterne is not yet in English. Of course the book has practically no text except for the back two pages, so any edition you can get your hands on is probably good enough. Udon Entertainment is releasing the Rei-covered Chronicle Illustrations English version on 12-11-12. They're also releasing Chronicle Side A and Side B sometime in 2013. I should also point out that 100% also has color plates of the original EVA series proposal, although the actual page images are not full size.

[]: Re: Evangelion Artbook Comparisons             Quote Post by AR-99 » Mon Dec 03, 2012 5:17 pm Hyper Shinchan wrote: Also I think that it's not been mentioned so far but there's also an updated release of Die Sterne in Japan

[]: Re: Evangelion Artbook Comparisons             Quote Post by pikadourei » Sat Dec 08, 2012 7:45 am AR-99 wrote: I have both versions. The original was in 2003, Ver. 2.0 was in 2007 and has 75 more illustrations, so if you want Die Sterne get Ver. 2.0, which is clearly marked as such but retains the Rei cover. Or if you're that type of collector, get both anyway. If I could I would not have gotten both, but it was timing. Very hard to go wrong with the Newtype 100% Collection since it collects the illustrations that were out at the time that Eva was originally released up through February 1997.

[]:             Quote Post by AR-99 » Sun Dec 09, 2012 8:04 pm Both Die Sternes have an image index which appears to be organized by artist. Thus any new illustrations done by x are in their section, making a side by side comparison of both volumes tricky because you would be going back and forth between indices. Thus I would probably say that you have probably seen the illustrations elsewhere for various media and that 2.0 is not the only place you can find them, but it's hard to nail down exactly where they else they may have appeared.

[]:             Quote Post by Hyper Shinchan » Mon Dec 10, 2012 9:18 am AR-99 wrote: it's hard to nail down exactly where they else they may have appeared.

[]:             Quote Post by AR-99 » Mon Dec 10, 2012 10:47 am I didn't know that the editors had taken the trouble of identifying where the artwork debuted, but yes, 2.0 has that information also.

Article: Evangelion Artbook Comparisons
. Die Sterne is not yet in English. Of course the book has practically no text except for the back two pages, so any edition you can get your hands on is probably good enough. Udon Entertainment is releasing the Rei-covered Chronicle Illustrations English version on 12-11-12. They're also releasing Chronicle Side A and Side B sometime in 2013. I should also point out that 100% also has color plates of the original EVA series proposal, although the actual page images are not full size.

The original was in 2003, Ver. 2.0 was in 2007 and has 75 more illustrations, so if you want Die Sterne get Ver. 2.0, which is clearly marked as such but retains the Rei cover. Or if you're that type of collector, get both anyway. If I could I would not have gotten both, but it was timing. Very hard to go wrong with the Newtype 100% Collection since it collects the illustrations that were out at the time that Eva was originally released up through February 1997.

Thus any new illustrations done by x are in their section, making a side by side comparison of both volumes tricky because you would be going back and forth between indices. Thus I would probably say that you have probably seen the illustrations elsewhere for various media and that 2.0 is not the only place you can find them, but it's hard to nail down exactly where they else they may have appeared.
